content: Phase 6 image sourcing — visual-verification hierarchy after SHA-1 byte verification: (a) 'confirmed' = ship name lettering directly visible on hull or verifiable class-signature (e.g., Edge-class orange Magic Carpet, NCL Encore Arranz-Bravo abstract hull art, Costa yellow C funnel); (b) 'consistent' = class matches Commons attribution but sister-class is visually indistinguishable (Lady-class, Solstice/Edge-class, Oceania R-class, Silversea Nova-class, MSC Lirica-class, Princess Grand/Royal/Sphere-class); (c) 'ambiguous' = disclosure only, no confirmation; (d) 'wrong' = subject rejected. SHA-1 proves bytes, not subject.
